$metadata.description: Kluvs Design System — canonical design tokens. Platform-agnostic source of truth. Compatible with Style Dictionary v4 ($value / $type / $description format). When syncing a client repo, read this file and propagate changed values to the platform-specific token files (Color.kt, Colors.swift, tailwind.config.js, etc.).

primary.$description: Copper — the only chromatic accent. One per view, on the primary CTA and active state only.

primary-hover.$description: Copper darkened for hover/press states on filled buttons.

primary-light.$description: Copper lightened — use sparingly for tinted backgrounds or soft highlights.

tertiary.$description: Blue-teal — admin role color, info accents.

on-primary.$description: Text/icon color on copper surfaces. Contrast vs primary: 3.53:1 — known exception, see accessibility notes.

warm-dark.$description: Warm near-black surface scale — the canonical dark surface stack for the product UI.

card.$description: Card surface — one step above base.

card-2.$description: Elevated card surface and hairline divider color.

accent-fill.$description: Tinted warm fill for the highlighted Next Discussion card.

foreground-warm.$description: Text and icon colors for use on warm dark surfaces.

primary.$description: Cream — primary body text and headings on dark. Contrast vs warm-dark base: 16.32:1 (AAA).

label.$description: Labels and subtitles on dark. Contrast vs warm-dark base: 10.26:1 (AAA).

tertiary.$description: Meta text and captions on dark. Contrast vs warm-dark base: 4.94:1 (AA).

placeholder.$description: Input placeholder text on dark. Contrast vs warm-dark base: 3.07:1 (AA UI).

disabled.$description: Disabled text on dark. Contrast vs warm-dark base: 1.90:1 — intentionally inaccessible to signal non-interactivity.

light.$description: Web and auth/marketing surfaces. Cool-neutral scale.

page.$description: Page background for auth and marketing screens.

divider.$description: Divider and border on light surfaces.

foreground-light.$description: Text colors for use on light surfaces.

primary.$description: Primary text on light. Contrast vs white: 17.40:1 (AAA).

secondary.$description: Secondary / supporting text on light. Contrast vs white: 5.74:1 (AA).

owner.$description: Mustard — Owner role badge. ~7:1 on dark, ~3:1 on light — usable as a graphical badge indicator on both surfaces.

admin.$description: Teal — Admin role badge. AA on light (6.45:1). Known exception on dark (2.95:1) — used as graphical indicator only, never body text.

danger.$description: Error / danger. AA on dark (5.06:1). Known exception on light (3.76:1) — always used alongside 'Error:' text prefix, never as sole state indicator.

primary-subtle.$description: Copper tinted fill — advisory/warning context boxes (e.g. 'This will disconnect Discord').

primary-border-soft.$description: Copper soft border — pairs with primary-subtle for warning box borders.

danger-subtle.$description: Danger tinted fill — destructive consequence boxes (e.g. 'This will permanently delete').

danger-border-soft.$description: Danger soft border — pairs with danger-subtle for consequence box borders.

dark-web.$description: Neutral dark surface stack (cool, not warm). Used in kluvs-frontend. Distinguish from the warm-dark stack which has brown-tinted near-blacks.

overlay.$description: Backdrop colors for modal dialogs and drawers.

spacing.$description: 4-unit base scale. Page gutters: space-5 (20px). Card padding: space-4 (16px).

modal.$description: Dialog and sheet container corner radius. Same value as radius.lg — semantic alias for platform engineers.

pill.$description: Avatars, bottom-nav pill, fully rounded elements.

default.$description: House identity easing — used for all transitions.

sans.$description: UI chrome, body text, labels, buttons, and all non-literary content. Loaded via Google Fonts CDN (wght 400;500;700).

serif.$description: Wordmark, display text, page headings, section headings, and book titles. Italic variant used for book titles specifically. Loaded via Google Fonts CDN (wght 400;500;700, italic 400;500). This is the literary register — use for content, not UI chrome.

tier.$description: Four-tier content hierarchy. Maps M3 type roles (mobile) to utility classes (web).

1-section-header.$description: Section labels — 'Current Book', 'Next Discussion'. Understated; guides the eye without dominating.

2-primary-content.$description: Most important information — club names, book titles, member names. Full contrast.

3-supporting-details.$description: Secondary context — counts, metadata, handles, dates. Lighter treatment.

4-fine-print.$description: Version numbers, disclaimers. Barely-there — accessible but not distracting.

scale.$description: Named type sizes. Web-canonical names match Tailwind config in kluvs-frontend.

modal-label.$description: Eyebrow-variant label used as the modal dialog title. Intentionally tighter (11px) and wider-tracked (0.14em) than the standard eyebrow (12px / 0.1em) — signals 'dialog mode' at a glance.

button.$description: Button system — five variants: Primary (filled), Outlined, Text/Ghost, Text Destructive, and Social (provider-branded OAuth). All M3 variants use colorScheme tokens and adapt to light/dark automatically. Social buttons have fixed provider colors and work on both surfaces.

text.$description: White on copper. 3.53:1 contrast — documented exception. Do not use colorScheme.background as text color.
Destructive action. Always paired with a cancel/dismiss text button.

loading-pattern.$description: Current pattern. Future: animated Kluvs mark SVG rotating.

modal.$description: Three-zone dialog shell — Header / Body / Footer (+ optional DangerZone between Body and Footer). Variants: default (copper eyebrow) and destructive (danger eyebrow). See docs/modal.md for full anatomy spec and platform notes.

label-color-default.$description: color.brand.primary — copper eyebrow for edit/info modals.

cancel-style.$value: ghost text, secondary color, hover → primary

action-style.$value: filled button (bg-primary or bg-danger), rounded-btn, text-white

loading-pattern.$value: KluvsSpinner size=14 color=#ffffff + '…' suffix on label

ui-kit-substitute.$description: ui_kits/mobile/ uses Lucide inline SVGs from the initial generation pass. Replace with Material Symbols exports when building production screens.
